concept_title: KEY CONCEPT 1: Science Starts with Curious Questions

experience.narration: Science begins when we feel curious and look carefully. A question is not a weakness. It is the first step of discovery. When we notice something and ask why or how, our mind starts searching for a true answer. (Clear concept image placed separately to avoid text overlap) bodies. His work reminds us that big discoveries can begin with a curious question. Wizkids Gurukula - Grade 6 Science Self-Study Lesson

content.video: What is Science? For Kids - The Scientific Method
content.meaning: A strong wish to know or understand something.
content.example: You see a flower open in the morning and ask, “Why did it open now?”
content.helpful_word: Observation — careful noticing using our senses.
content.read_write: Write one thing you saw today and one question about it.
content.observe: Look at a safe object near you for one minute. Notice its colour, shape, size, texture, or sound.
content.bharatiya_connection: Aryabhata studied the sky carefully and wrote about the movement of heavenly
content.local_life: Before monsoon rain, many families observe clouds and wind and ask when it may rain.
takeaway: Careful noticing helps us ask better questions.
Simple questions can lead to real learning.

concept_title: KEY CONCEPT 2: Science Is Everywhere Around Us

experience.narration: Science is not only in laboratories. It is in the kitchen, garden, sky, ocean, playground, and school bag. When we ask what things are made of, how water changes, why stars shine, or how plants grow, we are using science thinking. (Clear concept image placed separately to avoid text overlap) see. health. This shows how science questions can begin from daily life. questions. Wizkids Gurukula - Grade 6 Science Self-Study Lesson

content.video: Science All Around Us - Why Science Matters in Daily Life
content.meaning: Everything around us, such as air, water, plants, animals, objects, sky, and people.
content.example: Your classroom, home, garden, road, and playground are part of your surroundings.
content.read_write: Make four boxes: kitchen, sky, garden, school bag. Write one science question for each.
content.observe: Watch water in a glass, a plant near a window, or clouds before rain. Write only what you truly
content.bharatiya_connection: Charaka carefully observed food, plants, and the human body while writing about
content.local_life: In an Indian kitchen, boiling milk, steaming rice, and a pressure cooker can raise many science
takeaway: Science is present in daily life.
Every place can give us questions.
Nature and objects can both be studied.

concept_title: KEY CONCEPT 3: Finding Answers Step by Step

experience.narration: In science, we do not jump to the answer. We observe, ask a question, guess a possible answer, test the guess, and analyse what happened. This step-by-step way helps us solve small and big problems. (Clear concept image placed separately to avoid text overlap) science grows stronger when guesses are checked properly. Wizkids Gurukula - Grade 6 Science Self-Study Lesson

content.meaning: A step-by-step way to find answers to questions.
content.example: If a pen stops writing, we check the refill before deciding the pen is broken.
content.helpful_word: Experiment — a test or activity done to check an idea.
content.read_write: Write the five steps for the pen problem: observe, question, guess, test, analyse.
content.do_activity: Choose one safe classroom problem, such as a pencil point breaking. Think of two possible reasons.
content.bharatiya_connection: Jagadish Chandra Bose used careful experiments to test his ideas. He showed that
content.think_carefully: “The refill is empty” is a guess. Opening and checking the refill is a test.
takeaway: Testing helps us check a guess.
Analysing results helps us learn honestly.

concept_title: KEY CONCEPT 4: We Can All Work Like Scientists

experience.narration: A scientist uses careful questions, observations, tests, and thinking to find answers. But science is not only for famous people. A cook, repair person, student, farmer, or electrician can work like a scientist when they solve problems step by step. (Clear concept image placed separately to avoid text overlap) and work as a team. Science becomes stronger when people share ideas carefully. Wizkids Gurukula - Grade 6 Science Self-Study Lesson

content.meaning: A person who uses careful steps to discover or solve something.
content.example: A student checking why a seed did not grow is working like a scientist.
content.read_write: Write one question. Ask a friend to add one more observation to your question.
content.observe: With a partner, look at the same safe object for one minute. Compare what each of you noticed.
content.bharatiya_connection: ISRO missions such as Chandrayaan need many people to observe, test, improve,
content.local_life: A bicycle repair person checks where air leaks before fixing a flat tyre.
content.think_carefully: If your friend sees something different, check again before deciding.
takeaway: Anyone can think like a scientist.
Honesty and patience are important in science.
